topic.name: Sustainable Urban Development
topic.description: The topic encompasses strategies and practices aimed at creating environmentally friendly and socially inclusive urban spaces.

doc1: integration of green spaces in cities
doc2: reducing carbon footprint in metropolitan areas

topic.name: Sustainable Urban Development
topic.description: The topic focuses on strategies and practices for developing urban areas in an environmentally sustainable manner.

doc2: impact of urbanization on the environment
doc3: renewable energy solutions for urban areas

topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting renewable energy and reducing environmental impact.

doc2: highlights the importance of energy efficiency

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Reforms
topic.description: Legislation focused on amending the Social Security Act to improve Medicaid and Medicare services and coverage.

Document 1: requires drug manufacturers to pay rebates to state prescription drug discount programs
condition of participating in a Medicaid rebate agreement
Document 2: repeal the Medicare outpatient rehabilitation therapy caps
Document 3: extend and expand a temporary exception from certain Medicare payment limits
involves severe wounds and long-term care hospital services
Document 4: mandatory Medicaid coverage of drugs prescribed for certain research study child participants
regardless of Medicaid eligibility
Document 5: removes the limitation on payments by the Secretary of Health and Human Services
increases federal assistance for territories
Document 6: require payment for post-mastectomy external breast prosthesis garments
develop policies to ensure appropriate beneficiary access
Document 7: revise payment rules for graduate medical education costs
establishes a new medical residency training program

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Health and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to health services, flexible spending arrangements, and tax incentives for various public services.

Document 1: exempt fixed-wing aircraft used to provide acute care emergency medical services from certain restrictions on tax-exempt bond financing.
Document 2: allow a distribution to a participant in a flexible spending arrangement (FSA) of FSA funds not used to reimburse medical expenses by the end of the plan year.
Includes such distributions in the gross income of the FSA participant for income and employment tax purposes.
Document 3: revise the income eligibility level for home and community-based (HCBS) services for elderly and disabled individuals.
amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax deduction for premiums on qualified LTC insurance contracts.
Document 5: allow employers a business tax credit for 50 percent of the actual compensation paid to Ready Reserve-National Guard employees while on active duty.

topic.name: U.S. Tariff and Trade Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures related to the suspension, extension, or modification of tariffs on various goods to promote trade and manufacturing.

Document 1: Extends duty-free treatment, subject to specified quantitative limitations
Extends such duty-free treatment for 10 years beginning on January 1, 2015.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on nylon woolpacks
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to increase the duty on polyurethane sponges
Prescribes requirements for the duty treatment of steel wool cleaning pads.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing fluopyram and trifloxystrobin.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain knit-to-shape blouses and shirts of man-made fibers for girls.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on parts and accessories of optical instruments and apparatuses.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to: (1) revise the article description of paclobutrazol technical; and (2) extend the temporary suspension of duty on such article.

topic.name: U.S. Military Operations and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative measures related to the operation, management, and accountability of U.S. military forces and intelligence activities.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Air Force to maintain the current leadership rank, aircraft, and core functions
Prohibits the Secretary of the Air Force from changing the aircraft or core functions
Document 2: Prohibits operations involving the use of an armed unmanned aerial vehicle
Subjects the operation and use of armed UAVs to the requirements of laws applicable to the Armed Forces
Document 3: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the DNI to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of Defense to transfer such member to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for the receipt of such care
Directs that the VA-chosen care be provided at the VA facility closest to the permanent duty station
Document 7: Requires that amounts made available under the Iraq Security Forces Fund shall be provided as loans subject to full repayment
For each of FY2008-FY2009, the Secretary of Defense shall withhold from obligation and expenditure not less than 20% of the amount made available

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Tribes
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at extending federal recognition and associated benefits to specific Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina
Makes its members eligible for the services and benefits provided to Indians
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
Makes the tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government
Document 5: An Indian tribe or a tribal organization is defined as an eligible entity for grants
Document 7: Interior shall convey to Alaska, in exchange, 206 acres of federal land

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing the benefits and support systems available to veterans, including nutrition assistance, educational programs, burial rights, and retraining opportunities.

Document 1: expands eligibility for supplemental nutrition assistance (SNAP) benefits to include certain veterans
pending service-connected disability claims
Document 2: recodifies and revises federal veterans' benefits law concerning educational assistance
requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to carry out such assistance program
Document 3: makes eligible for burial in a national cemetery any member of the reserves
increases the Department of Veterans Affairs payment for funeral expenses
Document 4: directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to provide information concerning submitting a claim for compensation
outreach services provided to veterans
Document 6: authorizes the Secretary of Labor to pay an assistance allowance for each month such veteran is enrolled in an employment and training program
covers veterans who are unemployed for at least four consecutive months

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Military Families
topic.description: This cluster focuses on immigration legislation related to military personnel, their families, and refugees, addressing naturalization, enforcement, and resettlement processes.

Document 1: any person who serves or has served under honorable conditions as a member of the U.S. Armed Forces... shall be eligible for naturalization

exempts from worldwide immigrant visa numerical limitations an alien who is eligible for a family-sponsored immigrant visa and is either the spouse or child of a permanent resident alien who is serving in the Armed Forces.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to replace existing family-sponsored immigrant categories with a single preference allocation for spouses and children of permanent resident aliens.
Document 3: Authorizes: (1) states or their political subdivisions to enact and enforce immigration criminal penalties

Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to provide the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) of the Department of Justice (DOJ) with all information that the Secretary has regarding any alien.

topic.name: Consumer Safety and Drug Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the safety and regulation of consumer products and pharmaceuticals, focusing on ensuring public health and access to safe medications.

Document 1: Creating and Restoring Equal Access to Equivalent Samples Act of 2016 or the CREATES Act of 2016
permits the developer of a drug or biological product to bring a civil action against the license holder of an approved medication
Document 2: Requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to issue regulations requiring that a warning label stating that 'exposure to violent video games has been linked to aggressive behavior' be placed on the packaging of any video game
Document 3: Establishes in the Department of Commerce an Office of Imported and Domestic Product Safety
protects the health and safety of U.S. consumers
Document 4: Defines 'children's food or beverage container' as any container...primarily for consumption from that container by children
Requires that any children's food or beverage container that is composed in whole or in part of bisphenol A (BPA) be treated as a banned hazardous substance
Document 5: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise provisions regarding review and approval of generic drug applications
The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) must prioritize the review of such submissions and act on them within 180 days
Document 6: directs the Department of Health and Human Services to submit to the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) a recommendation to transfer marijuana from schedule I to another controlled substances schedule
The legislation amends the Controlled Substances Act (CSA) to exclude 'cannabidiol' (CBD) from the definition of 'marijuana'
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to provide that a food shall be deemed to be misbranded if it contains any spice, flavoring, or coloring derived from meat
unless its labeling bears a statement with appropriate prominence on the information panel

topic.name: Transportation Safety and Infrastructure
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety, infrastructure, and training in air and rail transportation systems.

Document 1: establish minimum dimensions for passenger seats
safety and health of passengers
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Transportation to designate high-speed rail infrastructure bonds
substantial contribution to providing the infrastructure required
Document 3: require air carriers to provide initial and annual recurring training for flight attendants
dealing with disruptive passengers
Document 4: use grant funds for intercity passenger rail service capital projects
for any other transportation projects
Document 5: promote passenger rail operations and transportation-oriented development
provide direct loans and loan guarantees for qualified rail projects
Document 6: suspends the authority of the FAA to realign air traffic control facilities
until January 1, 2009
Document 7: develop a national surface transportation system that advances the national interest
increase the total usage of public transportation, intercity passenger rail services

topic.name: Workforce Development Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing workforce development, unemployment compensation, and worker rights.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Labor to issue a final rule that allows a state to enact legislation to test an applicant for unemployment compensation for use of controlled substances.

Prescribes a special rule providing that, if the Secretary has not issued an interim final rule within 180 days after enactment of this Act, the final rule shall require the state unemployment compensation agency to determine whether an occupation regularly conducts drug testing.
Document 2: Requires foreign labor contractors and employers to inform foreign workers accurately of specified terms and conditions of their employment.
Prohibits discrimination in employment by an employer or a recruiter against an individual because of race, color, creed, sex, national origin, religion, age, or disability.
Document 3: Amends the Energy Employees Occupational Illness Compensation Program Act to include an employee of the Department of Energy for purposes of occupational illness compensation.
Provides that a claim that an individual qualifies for compensation or benefits under such Act shall be considered notwithstanding any denial of any other claim.
Document 4: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1988 to require the Secretary of Labor to establish the Unemployment Tuition Assistance Program.
Directs the Secretary to maintain a public Internet website containing Program information, including a list of participating community colleges.
Document 5: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act to extend emergency unemployment compensation payments for eligible individuals.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until June 30, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to deem approved any state laws that provide unemployment compensation to an individual for any week while in training.

Defines 'recognized postsecondary credential' as a credential consisting of an industry-recognized certificate, a certificate of completion of an apprenticeship, or an associate or baccalaureate degree.
Document 7: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to prohibit the NLRB from requiring an employer to provide employee telephone numbers or email addresses.

topic.name: Education Policy and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to educational funding, student safety, and support systems within schools.

Document 1: Protecting Student Athletes from Concussions Act of 2015
Each local educational agency must develop and implement a standard plan for concussion safety and management for its public schools.
Document 3: Amends the Impact Aid program
alters the formula for determining the payments due LEAs for federal ownership of property
Document 4: Need-Based Educational Aid Act of 2015

extends through FY2022 the antitrust exemption that allows institutions of higher education that admit all students on a need-blind basis to enter or attempt to enter into agreements among themselves regarding the administration of need-based financial aid.

topic.name: Small Business Support and Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing support, training, and opportunities for small businesses, particularly those owned by women and disadvantaged groups.

Document 2: require each Office of Small and Disadvantaged Business Utilization of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to assist a small business concern awarded a federal contract
provide assistance to improve the ability of proteges to compete for federal prime contracts
Document 3: direct the Office of Women's Business Ownership within the Small Business Administration (SBA) to address issues concerning the management, operations, manufacturing, technology, finance
provide financial assistance to qualifying entities to conduct projects designed to provide training and counseling meeting the needs of women business owners
Document 5: remove current contract award price limits
allow a contracting officer to award a sole source contract to any economically disadvantaged women-owned small business
Document 7: prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in the total amount of such loans outstanding at that credit union at any one time exceeding either: 1.75 times the actual net worth of the credit union
authorize the National Credit Union Administration Board to approve an application by an insured credit union to make one or more member business loans

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, accountability, and benefits of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Directs the Postmaster General to provide for the issuance of a forever stamp
to honor the sacrifices of the men and women of the Armed Forces
Document 2: Establishes as a nonprofit corporation the Post Office Consumer Action Group (POCAG)
to represent and promote the interests of individual residential postal users
Document 3: prohibits the U.S. Postal Service from requiring centralized delivery
for certain residential housing units
Document 4: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to make the Postmaster General the importer of record
for non-letter class mail imported into the United States
Document 5: Prescribes the 'average pay' and the appropriate percentage thereof to be used in determining annuities
for civilian employment with the United States Postal Service
Document 6: Makes procedures governing the closure or consolidation of postal branches
the same as those applicable to post offices
Document 7: directs the U.S. Postal Service to consider the economic effects on the community served by such post office
in making a determination whether to close or consolidate a post office

topic.name: Voting Rights and Election Integrity
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting voting rights, ensuring election integrity, and facilitating access to the voting process for various groups, including military personnel and overseas voters.

Document 1: Protect American Democracy Act of 2016 directs actions against foreign interference in U.S. elections.
Prohibits listed foreign persons from receiving U.S. entry visas and revokes current visas.
Document 2: Military Voting Protection Act of 2009 establishes procedures for absentee ballots for overseas uniformed services voters.
Ensures ballots are delivered prior to the closing of polls.
Document 4: Voter Confidence and Increased Accessibility Act of 2009 revises audit requirements for voting systems.
Requires use of durable, voter-verified paper ballots.
Document 5: Universal Right to Vote by Mail Act of 2010 prohibits states from imposing additional conditions on mail-in voting eligibility.
Ensures accessibility to voting by mail for federal elections.
Document 7: Military and Overseas Voting Enhancement Act requires states to accept absentee ballots from overseas voters.
Mandates reimbursement for overseas voters using express mail services to transmit ballots.

topic.name: Child and Family Health Policies
topic.description: Legislation focused on protecting children's rights and health in various contexts, including privacy, education, and reproductive health.

Document 1: Amends the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act of 1998
Establishes additional privacy protections against the collection of personal or geolocation information from children and minors.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive, matching Quality Pathways grants to states
Requires the grant to be used to further such progress and move more disadvantaged children into higher quality programs.
Document 3: Prohibition against the federal government penalizing health care providers based on refusal to provide coverage for abortion
Investigate complaints alleging discrimination based on an individual's religious belief.
Document 4: Disqualify amounts paid for an abortion for tax deduction purposes
Permits the purchase of separate abortion coverage if premium assistance tax credits are not used.
Document 5: Physical examination shall be performed by a VA-certified health care provider
Establish a system to share data and maintain the medical and personnel records.
Document 6: Adds an exception for cases where the pregnancy is the result of rape or incest
Repeals a statutory restriction on using a medical treatment facility to perform an abortion.
Document 7: Amends the school improvement program to require each state to create a positive school climate
Requires schools to provide parents with the number of incidents of school violence, bullying, and disciplinary actions.

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Jurisdiction
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts that address jurisdictional issues, rights, and protections in different contexts including marriage, water rights, public prayer, employment, and education.

Document 1: Prohibits federal courts from having jurisdiction to adjudicate or enforce any claim pertaining to the validity, under the U.S. Constitution, of a state law or a state administrative or judicial decision that defines marriage as limited to the union of one man and one woman.
Allows final judgments entered by federal courts before the enactment of this Act to remain binding on the parties to the case.
Document 4: Denies the Supreme Court and district courts jurisdiction to review any matter related to the alleged establishment of religion involving the expression of public prayer by any federal, state, or local government entity.
This Act limits judicial review in matters of public prayer.
Document 5: Prohibits employers from requiring or requesting that employees or applicants for employment provide their passwords or any other means for accessing their private email accounts or personal online accounts.
Employers who violate these prohibitions are subject to civil penalties.

topic.name: Federal Environmental and Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating environmental standards, promoting renewable energy, and managing federal resources.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Power Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to review any decision by the North American Electric Reliability Corporation (NAERC)
Requires FERC to publish a cost-benefit analysis of the proposed NAERC decision.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to require the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to specify performance criteria for monitoring coastal recreation waters
Authorizes appropriations for grants to states and local governments for developing and implementing monitoring and notification programs.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to establish a program under which the Secretary shall provide competitive grants to states, Indian tribes, and local governments to provide rebates, loans, or other incentives to eligible participants for the purchase and installation of solar energy systems
Requires a grant recipient to certify that funds will be used to supplement, expand, or create new programs and to deploy an increased quantity of solar energy systems.

topic.name: Insurance and Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to various forms of insurance, social security, and support for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Requires an insurance issuer that offers a long-term care insurance plan to implement claims dispute resolution procedures
allows an enrollee to appeal the decision of an independent reviewer to an appropriate state court
Document 2: Requires an employer to notify an employee in writing upon the purchase of an employer-owned insurance policy
violation of this Act constitutes an unfair method of competition
Document 3: Authorizes the Attorney General to maintain public databases for missing persons
establish a matching grant program to assist in the reporting of missing persons
Document 4: directs the Department of Veterans Affairs to study and report on programs to assist veterans in their transition to civilian life
determine the effectiveness of current programs, especially concerning the challenges faced by women veterans
Document 5: Amends the National Housing Act to make exceptions to the prohibition against mortgage insurance
Makes eligible for mortgage insurance, in spite of a seller-financed downpayment
Document 6: amends title II of the Social Security Act to establish specified new disability classifications
require periodic continuing disability reviews for beneficiaries classified as 'medical improvement possible'
Document 7: Establishes the Financial Oversight Commission to investigate the financial crisis of 2008
directs the Commission to identify, review, and evaluate the lessons learned from the crisis

topic.name: Courtroom and Data Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to courtroom procedures, media coverage, and the protection of personal information in legal contexts.

Document 1: Requires the Supreme Court to permit television coverage of all open sessions of the Court
unless it decides by majority vote that allowing such coverage in a particular case would violate the due process rights
Document 2: Prohibits a business from transferring personally identifiable information of a U.S. citizen to any foreign affiliate
without providing notice to such citizen that the information may be transferred
Document 3: Authorizes the presiding judge of a U.S. appellate court or U.S. district court to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising to the public of court proceedings
except when such action would constitute a violation of the due process rights of any party
Document 4: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring commercial entities to implement information security policies
Establishes procedures to be followed in the event of an information security breach

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts and Oversight
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. legislative acts aimed at improving safety, emergency response, and international relations while emphasizing oversight and funding mechanisms.

Document 1: directs the President, through the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), to initiate a review of known instances since 2011
ascertain factors that may have undermined efforts to prevent such travel
Document 2: provides for matching grants (with a federal share of up to 80%) to eligible entities for the implementation and operation of E-911 services
training in 9-1-1 services of public safety personnel
Document 3: direct the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) to develop an independent safety assessment procedure for nuclear facilities
a final NRC decision on whether to extend an operating license shall not be made until the NRC has completed the independent safety assessment
Document 4: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities
support the establishment and maintenance of interdisciplinary geriatric mental health outreach teams
Document 5: modifies the project for flood damage reduction
requires the nonfederal interests to receive credit for expenses incurred
Document 6: reauthorize the program for the completion of appraisals and satisfaction of other legal requirements for the sale or exchange of public land
establish and maintain a database containing a comprehensive list of all of the public land identified for disposal
Document 7: prohibits any amounts from being obligated or expended to provide assistance to the Palestinian Authority until it withdraws its request to join the International Criminal Court

topic.name: Government Infrastructure and Safety Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures focused on improving infrastructure, safety, and communication systems within government frameworks.

Document 1: Renames the Department of Defense (DOD) regional center for security studies
Asia-Pacific Center for Security Studies.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement an expedited program
to examine and determine the validity of each unpatented mining claim.
Document 3: Establishes the Infrastructure Financing Authority (IFA)
to facilitate the construction, consolidation, alteration, or repair of transportation, water, and energy infrastructure projects.
Document 4: Requires the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to establish a program of research, development, and demonstration
for mine communications.
Document 5: Directs the Attorney General to establish a national Silver Alert communications network
to assist regional and local search efforts for missing seniors.
Document 6: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act
to improve access to local foods by schools.
Document 7: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
to establish the Stronger Together Program for improving diversity in public schools.

topic.name: Environmental and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the conservation, restoration, and management of natural resources and habitats in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes in the Treasury the National Endowment for the Oceans which shall fund grants for programs to restore, maintain, or understand living marine resources and their habitats
Directs the Secretary to establish a National Endowment for the Oceans Council.
Document 2: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management in Humboldt County, Nevada, as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Sets forth provisions for the management of such land, including regarding livestock grazing, water rights, and fish and wildlife management.
Document 4: Requires the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a California New River restoration program
Provide grants and technical assistance for coordinating restoration and protection activities.
Document 6: Reauthorizes estuary habitat restoration projects and the Environmental Protection Agency's Great Lakes Restoration Initiative through FY2021
Establishes requirements for navigation and flood management projects, reservoir operations, and aquatic invasive species.
